title: 5.0 SAIL - QC Open Point and Technical Queries

content.text: This page is to summarize the Open Points for SAIL bring up

rows: To flash out generated elf whether any secure or safety logic has to be added to our generated elf If yes, we need to know the logic to generate a proper elf file which will be loaded and executed by Hypervisor Because when we flash our built ELF file we are able to see Hypervisor is not loading the elf file flashed by us
CLOSE
need sectool to sign the elf. startup address is EL1 reset address (El1 entry)
Which documents from Qualcomm explains the details of peripherals attached to SAIL and its corresponding block diagram and register information (Technical reference manual)
BLOCKER
Case 07436352 QC not release datasheet QC guy said this is confidential document

When we configure Inteerupts from EL1 SW, we see the execution always stuck in hypervisor. Two problems: Problem 1: When we check the GIC, we see an interrupt continously occuring → we have no idea what kind of interrupt is this Problem 2: Whenever the ISR occurs, the execution goes to hypervisor and EL1 ISR vector address is never called. (When hypervisor is active, EL1 SW can never receive the interrupt)
CLOSE
80-42846-209 Rev  SAIL Interrupts for QAM8650, QAM8255, and QAM8775 EL1 exception error → handle over by hypervisor → hypervisor do something detail issue future → case to QC

How the know the AR version released by Qualcomm and its relevant Hypervisor image. We see the MCAL from QC is using Hypervisor calls. So is there any version match required ? When we call Mcu_Init, the execution enter hypervisor and never returns We are using snapdragon-auto-hqx-4-5-6-0_hlos_dev_autosar-refs_heads_qcom_init_es07_r00009.1 SAIL version to build our SW. And our target is QAM8255, can you share the matching Hypervisor for the autosar version (we need single binary for all code, not separate elf for each core)
CLOSE

autosar hypervisor version in test_device_autosar https://chipcode.qti.qualcomm.com/robert-bosch-gmbh/snapdragon-auto-hqx-4-5-6-0_test_device_autosar/tree/r00008.1 EL1 exception error → handle over by hypervisor → hypervisor do something QC updated document(Case 07452335 ) How+to+flash+SAIL+AutoSAR.pdf pinor_QC guys provideds.7z
We see the HYP is configuring MPU based on partition for each core How to change these settings. Our generated elf is bigger in size and Hypervisor MPU is blocking the execution our flashed SW
CLOSE

MPU config is in DTS after can config DTS,can do next need test after properly nosym.elf released → we can modify the node existed in DTS. but could not add new node(not existed in DTS) refer to No.15

To debug the SW from start code in EL SW, is there a Hypervisor which can wait for us to connect the Lauterbach and after changing the register, the hypervisor jump to EL SW This will help to found issues in startup phase
CLOSE
method 1,  need QC provide special sailhyp.elf (MCAL User Manual, Chapter 2.3) sideload sailhyp provided method 2,  while( xxx) in main(), modify xxx  false when we need next step
how to build hyp with dts?
CLOSE

need setup QC environment (Mcal User Manual 80-42846-147_REV_AA_SAIL_EL2_Reference_chapter 2.4 ) OEMs can only build the lemansau-sailhyp.dts. Below are the commands. $cd sail_proc\build\ms $make sailhypdts. complex cfg need raise case.
How to QC debug SAIL?
CLOSE
same with us： Modify  source code-> build& generate elf -> flash by PCAT -> attach T32 script: http://sghkvm0011.apac.bosch.com/rb-vcupro_main_dev/xref/qnx/qcom_boot/common/Core/t32/ t32start.py

How to build sailhyp.elf with local autosar dts? now,  can local build safeRTOS version and replace sailhyp.elf of vcu pro daily,  flash it into SAIL, work well. maybe need autosar version sailhyp_nosym.elf , but not found.
CLOSE
autosar version sailhyp_nosym.elf, QC begin have in ES08 https://chipcode.qti.qualcomm.com/robert-bosch-gmbh/snapdragon-auto-hqx-4-5-6-0_hlos_dev_autosar/tree/r00010.1
ES06 (end to ES08) MCAL MemAcc some Autosar API required by FEE is not available Blankcheck and compare API detailed wiki : SAIL - FEE mismatch with QC Mem
ONGOING
Case 07488998 AUTORFI to QC, waiting their reply raised a new case for tracking Case 07557017 : AutoRFI for MemAcc(follow up Case 07488998) – 2024.11.25 CCU Fusion Paused – 2024.12.2

What's the difference of  dev_autosar, test_device_autosar; dev_sail, test_device; amss_standard_oem in chipcode 4.5.6.0 my understanding, test_device_autosar is test repo for dev_autosar; test_device is test repo for dev sail; what the difference about sailhyp_nosym.elf of amss_standard_oem and dev_device is which sailhyp_nosym.elf should customer used, to build sailhyp.elf with customer's *.dts?
CLOSE

Case : 07498208 test_device 代表的是编译好的镜像释放给客户，ANSS_STANDARD_OEM 代表的是整个源码目录，boot相关的，前者就是编译好的镜像你们可以刷机，后者就是纯源码。 另外snapdragon-auto-hqx-4-5-6-0_test_device_autosar/sail_autosar/sail_proc/build/ms/bin/SAIL_CASAR/DEBUG/lemans 这个下面应该有sailhyp_nosym.elf
How to run the sail program on DDR?
ONGOING

Case 07496473 You can directly use the ddr memory defined in the MCAL userguide and don't need consider this "wait-for-DDR" for your usercase. sail xbl load image to sram, but if you define the el1 image memory at ddr range, it can load it to that part, however it must wait for DDR ready, so it needs to set that dts flag "wait-for-DDR" to be 1. as mentioned in the document: SAIL EL1 Image load to DDR imageLoadToDDR { wait-for-DDR = <0>; //This wait-for-DDR flag is used for the specific scenario of loading EL1 images to MD DDR (executing them from DDR). When set to 1, the SAILHYP elf loader will delay EL1 image loading until MD DDR is powered up and ready. }; need test after nosym.elf released.
[8775 SAIL]how to configure SAIL_IO as PWM and ICU MCAL module PWM and ICU is missing from QC → we need this MCAL package
ONGOING

Case 07519234 2024.11.15 workaround by general clock -> 7.0 SAIL clock generation and edge detection ICU is ongoing autoRFI also raised To QTI — Waiting ICU also work properly by modify registers , now one thing also need to track: MPU Access for clock/ICU registers
How to use Hvc_Call for an API in Autosar version project? (How EL1 SWC modify EL2 protected area?)
BLOCKER

Case 07543748 Confirmed: EL1 SWC modify EL2 protected area, need modify EL2(hypervisor software) QTI not support PWM in SAIL; their also not support Customized sail hypervisor. So, USS 500Khz Clock have to be generated by other domain. e.g. MCU or MD and, SAIL have no capabilities of PWM (need discuss once sail development restart)

In ES6 release only one mailbox channel is given for User ( And even that channel is mentioned as Demo free channel ), all other channels are QTI reserved. We need at least two channel to transmit and receive. The Customer wants mailbox to transmit user date between MD and SAIL So query is, can we use mailbox for any user specific data transition between MD and SAIL
OBSOLETE
LIU Dezhi (XC-CP/ESW2-CN) Please raise a QC ticket for this Currently not raise case. Firstly, Test mailbox communication with MD LIU Dezhi (XC-CP/ESW2-CN)

Got properly sailhyp_nosym.elf and corresponding dts. Modified MPU config defined in DTS, worked properly; But when try to modify MPU region no defined in DTS, e.g. (0xF1100000 - F1304000, register region) → generate sailhyp.elf → flashed target, stucked in hypervisor.
CLOSE

Case 07547224 case raised, no reply yet. – 2024.11.19 Bosch Question: Got properly sailhyp_nosym.elf and corresponding dts. Modified MPU config defined in DTS, worked properly; But when try to modify MPU region no defined in DTS, e.g. (0xF1100000 - F1304000 register region) → generate sailhyp.elf → flashed target, stucked in hypervisor. 1. please help to find the reason. 2, what the difference of sailsw1_mem_map/el1_shared_mem_map/el2_mem_map, when I add a region, which type should I add in? 3, What the "shareable", "attribute", "region-enable" in Node_* mean? 4. when debug with T32, there has Memory Protection Unit PL1 and PL2, may region no defined in DTS here. could you describe the two part to us? QC answer: i recommend you refer to three documents to get to kown the memory map related informations: 80-70890-1 SAIL Memory Map 80-42846-146 SAIL_MCAL_User_Guide SAIL EL2 Reference Application Note (80-42846-147) For the mpu parameters like shareable/attruibute. you can refer to arm website. just take an example, for sharable means : the context of the ARM Cortex-R52 processor, the MPU (Memory Protection Unit) Outer Shareable attribute indicates that the memory region can be shared with external components outside the processor core. This means that the memory region is accessible by other processors or devices in the system, ensuring data consistency across these components The MPU settings for different privilege levels (PL1 and PL2) are usually managed by the system software to control access permissions and memory attributes for different regions.These settings are often applied during system initialization or by the operating system, rather than being statically defined in the DTS. The sailhyp statically implements 18 EL2 MPU regions, EL1 MPU configuration is EL1 image responsibility and below EL1 configuration, supported through a device tree, needs to be adapted so the EL2 MPU config can be adapted. So the EL2 MPU regions not able for customer to modify by dts. project Paused — 2024.12.2

Bosch Question: Got properly sailhyp_nosym.elf and corresponding dts. Modified MPU config defined in DTS, worked properly; But when try to modify MPU region no defined in DTS, e.g. (0xF1100000 - F1304000 register region) → generate sailhyp.elf → flashed target, stucked in hypervisor. 1. please help to find the reason. 2, what the difference of sailsw1_mem_map/el1_shared_mem_map/el2_mem_map, when I add a region, which type should I add in? 3, What the "shareable", "attribute", "region-enable" in Node_* mean? 4. when debug with T32, there has Memory Protection Unit PL1 and PL2, may region no defined in DTS here. could you describe the two part to us?

QC answer: i recommend you refer to three documents to get to kown the memory map related informations: 80-70890-1 SAIL Memory Map 80-42846-146 SAIL_MCAL_User_Guide SAIL EL2 Reference Application Note (80-42846-147) For the mpu parameters like shareable/attruibute. you can refer to arm website. just take an example, for sharable means : the context of the ARM Cortex-R52 processor, the MPU (Memory Protection Unit) Outer Shareable attribute indicates that the memory region can be shared with external components outside the processor core. This means that the memory region is accessible by other processors or devices in the system, ensuring data consistency across these components The MPU settings for different privilege levels (PL1 and PL2) are usually managed by the system software to control access permissions and memory attributes for different regions.These settings are often applied during system initialization or by the operating system, rather than being statically defined in the DTS. The sailhyp statically implements 18 EL2 MPU regions, EL1 MPU configuration is EL1 image responsibility and below EL1 configuration, supported through a device tree, needs to be adapted so the EL2 MPU config can be adapted. So the EL2 MPU regions not able for customer to modify by dts.

Bosch Question: Got properly sailhyp_nosym.elf and corresponding dts. Modified MPU config defined in DTS, worked properly; But when try to modify MPU region no defined in DTS, e.g. (0xF1100000 - F1304000 register region) → generate sailhyp.elf → flashed target, stucked in hypervisor. 1. please help to find the reason. 2, what the difference of sailsw1_mem_map/el1_shared_mem_map/el2_mem_map, when I add a region, which type should I add in? 3, What the "shareable", "attribute", "region-enable" in Node_* mean? 4. when debug with T32, there has Memory Protection Unit PL1 and PL2, may region no defined in DTS here. could you describe the two part to us?

QC answer: i recommend you refer to three documents to get to kown the memory map related informations: 80-70890-1 SAIL Memory Map 80-42846-146 SAIL_MCAL_User_Guide SAIL EL2 Reference Application Note (80-42846-147) For the mpu parameters like shareable/attruibute. you can refer to arm website. just take an example, for sharable means : the context of the ARM Cortex-R52 processor, the MPU (Memory Protection Unit) Outer Shareable attribute indicates that the memory region can be shared with external components outside the processor core. This means that the memory region is accessible by other processors or devices in the system, ensuring data consistency across these components The MPU settings for different privilege levels (PL1 and PL2) are usually managed by the system software to control access permissions and memory attributes for different regions.These settings are often applied during system initialization or by the operating system, rather than being statically defined in the DTS. The sailhyp statically implements 18 EL2 MPU regions, EL1 MPU configuration is EL1 image responsibility and below EL1 configuration, supported through a device tree, needs to be adapted so the EL2 MPU config can be adapted. So the EL2 MPU regions not able for customer to modify by dts.
600KB autosarsail.elf is not work properly with multi-core sailhyp.elf(released in case 07529936), stuck in hypervisor
CLOSE

Case 07547186 case raised, no reply yet. -2024.11.19 some cases same multi-core sailhyp.elf + 600Kb elf + local 8775 HW , work properly same multi-core sailhyp.elf + 600Kb elf + nassy 8775 HW , cant work properly same multi-core sailhyp.elf + 600Kb elf + VCU Pro 8255 HW , cantwork properly new multi-core sailhyp.elf (delay 3s in sailhyp.elf)+ 600Kb elf + VCU Pro 8255HW , work properly new multi-core sailhyp.elf + 600Kb elf +CCU Fusion HW , work properly
[8775] what SAIL doing when 8775 MD enter STR
CLOSE
Case 07580357 当MD进STR的时候，​SAIL会关闭各种监控，最后进入Sleep状态（Low Power , put CPU in WFI) 当SOC被唤醒，退出STR时，SAIL会Warn Rest,然后开启各种监控，从而正常工作。
Can QC provide Technical Reference Manual(include subsystem architecture, registers .etc) PWM ( no this document, we have no choice but raise case to QC) ICU
Any Sail training?
Hypervisor is black box, EL1 Error → jump to EL2， need tell us how to analysis In case of any exception or error → the control jumps to EL2 and we have no clue about the issue root cause
